feedback.text: I'd like the EU to look into the current state of videogames when the support by their publisher/owner ends, in particular, being able to play the games you have payed for when the developers decide it's time to move on or shut down the servers. I have played the videogame Destiny 2 for thousands of hours, this game has the particularity of following a "season" model. You pay for each season (around 40-60), and each season of the year the game introduces new content to keep it fresh, and this is fantastic, but in the past years they started taking out parts of the game due to "file size constraint issues", which superficially might seem reasonable, but this effectively made all content removed completely unplayable, including parts of the game with no need for multiplayer or connection to a server, such as history missions. In this case I'd like to point out that I had payed 60 for a part of the game that I enjoyed very much, but when the publishers/developers decided, they took out a major part of the game in exchange for the new content but NOT FOR FREE. The new parts I needed to pay for, which is fine, but I am now unable to play any of the missions/activities that I loved (and payed for!), without having had any agency over this decision. I request the EU sets up legislation to force video game companies to release in any reasonable capacity the ability to replay the content that they have decided to take out, whether this is open-sourcing the original code, or setting up some "fallback" servers so I can enjoy the product that I payed for, for as long as I want to. I believe video game companies should not have the ability to take my purchase for me. For context I have spent upwards of 200 for expansions and DLCs for Destiny 2 content (excluding in game coins, etc.), of which, as of today, I can't play 75% of it, it's flat out not there. I find this is a problem. Thanks.
